commit.message:
fix: timezone-aware auto clock-out and payroll total_hours correction

- Auto clock-out now respects each business's timezone: added timezone
  column to business_time_settings (default Africa/Johannesburg) with
  migration 101. Scheduler runs hourly and checks if it's the day_end
  hour (default 5 AM) in each business's local timezone.
- day_end_time default changed from 23:59 to 05:00
- should_auto_clock_out now checks current_time.hour == day_end_time.hour
  (triggers once per hour window, not perpetually after)
- total_hours in payroll report = net hours worked (breaks already
  deducted). Frontend and Excel export no longer double-subtract breaks.
- Migration updates existing rows: day_end_time 23:59→05:00, penalty 5→4

commit.message:
consensus: Fix select_leader to use sampling_weight per protocol §5.2/§9.2

reward_weight() (1:5:20:60) was incorrectly used for VRF leader selection.
Protocol §5.2 and §9.2 require sampling_weight() (1:10:100:1000).

A Gold node should have 1000x selection probability over Free, not 60x.
No impact on current all-Free testnet, but would diverge when paid tiers join.

Also fix stale comment in TimeProof: Q_finality is 67% not 51%.

commit.message:
fix(adversarial): raise L1 pass threshold to 8 when agent wrote real files

When an agent used code_write/code_edit (actual work), soft quality
issues like Overclaim or Contradiction on the description text (not
HALLUCINATION/SLOP/MOCK/LIES/ECHO/FAKE) should not block the cycle.
Raise threshold from 6 to 8 in that case.

Rationale: ac-architect writes INCEPTION.md (documentation), the L1
reviewer scores 7/10 for 'Overclaim' (agent claims file has all
elements, reviewer can't verify because code_read wasn't called).
This fix unblocks cycles that do real work but have imprecise text.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 7.1: AUTO-DETECTION & FALLBACK

Implement automatic GPU detection with software rendering fallback per
ROADMAP Phase 7.1. All three rendering paths (Intel GPU, AMD GPU, Software)
now accessible via unified interface with automatic selection.

Selection logic:
1. If ForceSoftware flag set → use software renderer
2. Attempt GPU detection at /dev/dri/renderD128
3. If Intel GPU (Gen9-Xe) detected → BackendIntelGPU
4. If AMD GPU (RDNA1-3) detected → BackendAMDGPU
5. If no GPU or init fails → BackendSoftware fallback

Implementation:
- Created Renderer interface for unified backend API
  * Render() and RenderWithDamage() for display list execution
  * Present() for DMA-BUF export (GPU) or error (software)
  * Dimensions() and Destroy() for resource management

- Implemented NewRenderer() auto-detection function
  * Queries GPU via render.DetectGPU()
  * Maps GPU generation to backend type
  * Creates appropriate backend or falls back to software
  * Supports ForceSoftware override for testing

- Created SoftwareBackend wrapper
  * Wraps SoftwareConsumer for unified interface
  * Implements damage tracking for incremental updates
  * Returns -1 from Present() (software has no DMA-BUF)
  * Provides Buffer() method for direct pixel access

- Added auto-render-demo binary
  * Demonstrates all three rendering paths
  * Tests auto-detection and fallback logic
  * Supports FORCE_SOFTWARE=1 environment override

Testing:
- All 21 new backend tests passing (100% pass rate)
  * Interface contract validation
  * Auto-detection with various GPU types
  * Software backend functionality
  * Invalid dimension handling
- All existing tests still passing
- Static linking verified (ldd: "not a dynamic executable")

Metrics:
- 8 complexity improvements vs 4 regressions (improving trend)
- Quality score: 38.1/100
- Zero critical issues in new code
- Regressions in unrelated files (x11-dmabuf-demo, wayland/shm)

Files modified:
- internal/render/backend/interface.go (new, 64 lines)
- internal/render/backend/auto.go (new, 145 lines)
- internal/render/backend/auto_test.go (new, 195 lines)
- internal/render/backend/software.go (new, 133 lines)
- internal/render/backend/software_test.go (new, 178 lines)
- cmd/auto-render-demo/main.go (new, 118 lines)
- ROADMAP.md (Phase 7.1 marked complete)

commit.message:
Fix invisible dirty indicator and stale git status after save in file editor (#524)

- [x] Fix Bug 1: Replace undefined `bg-ctp-peach`/`text-ctp-peach` with
`bg-ctp-warning`/`text-ctp-warning` in `TabBar.ts`
- [x] Fix Bug 1: Replace `bg-ctp-peach` with `bg-ctp-warning` in
`FileViewer.ts`
- [x] Fix Bug 2: Call `fileState.triggerRefresh()` after successful file
save in `FileViewer.ts`
- [x] Fix e2e test `find-replace.spec.ts`: update `.bg-ctp-peach`
selectors to `.bg-ctp-warning` to match renamed CSS class

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>[LOW] No dirty indicator on tabs; file tree not updated
after save</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Description
> 
> Two related bugs in the files plugin editor:
> 
> 1. **No dirty file indicator in tab** — When editing a file, the tab
does not show the peach dot indicator for unsaved changes. The dot
element renders but is invisible because the Tailwind class
`bg-ctp-peach` does not exist in the `@theme` block.
> 2. **File tree git status not updated after save** — After saving a
file (Cmd+S), the file tree does not show the file as modified (`M`
badge). The save handler writes to disk and clears dirty state, but
never signals the file tree to re-fetch git status.
> 
> > Possibly a duplicate of / related to Agent-Clubhouse/Clubhouse#510
(undefined Catppuccin color classes).
> 
> ## Root Cause
> 
> ### Bug 1: Invisible dirty dot
> 
> `TabBar.ts` uses `bg-ctp-peach` for the dirty indicator dot, but
`ctp-peach` is **not defined** in `src/renderer/index.css`'s `@theme`
block. The only defined color utilities are the semantic ones
(`ctp-warning`, `ctp-error`, `ctp-info`, `ctp-success`, `ctp-accent`,
plus the base/surface/text palette). Since `bg-ctp-peach` produces no
CSS output, the dot renders as an invisible element.
> 
> **Code pointers:**
> 
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/TabBar.ts:174` — pinned tab
dirty dot uses `bg-ctp-peach`
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/TabBar.ts:224` — regular tab
dirty dot uses `bg-ctp-peach`
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/TabBar.ts:216` — close button
uses `text-ctp-peach` (also undefined)
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileViewer.ts:385` — file header
dirty dot likely uses same undefined class
> - `src/renderer/index.css:6-19` — `@theme` block only defines semantic
colors, not raw Catppuccin names
> 
> ### Bug 2: File tree git status not refreshed after save
> 
> When a file is saved via `handleSave` or `saveFile`, the handler
writes the file via IPC and clears the tab's dirty flag, but **never
calls `fileState.triggerRefresh()`**. The file tree relies on a
file-watcher subscription with a 500ms debounce to eventually refresh
git status, but this is unreliable because:
> - The app's own IPC write may not trigger the watcher consistently
> - Even when it does, the 500ms debounce introduces a noticeable lag
> - No explicit signal connects save completion → tree git status reload
> 
> **Code pointers:**
> 
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileViewer.ts:251-264` —
`saveFile()`: writes file, clears dirty, but no `triggerRefresh()`
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileViewer.ts:275-289` —
`handleSave()`: same pattern, no `triggerRefresh()`
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/state.ts:404-407` —
`triggerRefresh()` exists but is never called from save paths
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileTree.ts:480-495` —
subscribes to `refreshCount` to reload git status
> - `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileTree.ts:509-522` — file
watcher debounce (500ms) is the only current trigger
> 
> ## Suggested Fix
> 
> ### Bug 1
> Replace `bg-ctp-peach` / `text-ctp-peach` with a defined semantic
color. `bg-ctp-warning` (yellow/gold) is the closest semantic match for
"unsaved changes":
> 
> ```typescript
> // TabBar.ts line 174 (pinned tab dirty dot)
> - className: 'absolute top-1 right-1 w-1.5 h-1.5 rounded-full
bg-ctp-peach',
> + className: 'absolute top-1 right-1 w-1.5 h-1.5 rounded-full
bg-ctp-warning',
> 
> // TabBar.ts line 224 (regular tab dirty dot)
> - className: 'w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-ctp-peach',
> + className: 'w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-ctp-warning',
> 
> // TabBar.ts line 216 (close button text when dirty)
> - 'text-ctp-peach hover:bg-ctp-surface1'
> + 'text-ctp-warning hover:bg-ctp-surface1'
> ```
> 
> ### Bug 2
> Call `triggerRefresh()` after a successful file save to immediately
update the tree:
> 
> ```typescript
> // FileViewer.ts saveFile() — after line 260
> fileState.triggerRefresh();
> 
> // FileViewer.ts handleSave() — after line 280
> fileState.triggerRefresh();
> ```
> 
> ## Files
> 
> | File | Lines | Issue |
> |------|-------|-------|
> | `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/TabBar.ts` | 174, 216, 224 |
Uses undefined `bg-ctp-peach` / `text-ctp-peach` |
> | `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileViewer.ts` | 251-264,
275-289, 385 | Missing `triggerRefresh()` after save; undefined color
class |
> | `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/state.ts` | 404-407 |
`triggerRefresh()` exists but unused from save paths |
> | `src/renderer/plugins/builtin/files/FileTree.ts` | 480-495, 509-522
| Only refreshes on watcher/manual, not on save signal |
> | `src/renderer/index.css` | 6-19 | `@theme` missing `ctp-peach`
definition |</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

feat(scripts): FR-138 add copilot session GC script (#17)

Add scripts/copilot_session_gc.sh to prune stale Copilot CLI sessions
from ~/.copilot/session-state/ based on age.

Features:
- --max-age DAYS (default: 7) controls age threshold
- --dry-run lists candidates without deleting
- --verbose shows kept sessions
- Protects active session via $COPILOT_SESSION_ID
- Exits cleanly when directory is missing
- Idempotent (safe to run repeatedly)
- Ignores non-directory files in session-state/

14 unit tests covering age filtering, dry-run, active session
protection, edge cases, and logging.

Co-authored-by: Test <test@test.com>
